act1.title: Audience Discovery
act1.question: Do our subscribers even drink canned coffee?
act1.context: Ember connects their Shopify + Meta data. LCBM analyzes 40K subscribers for RTD purchase propensity.

bridge_segment.name: Gateway Segment
bridge_segment.description: Subscribers who also buy sparkling water and energy drinks
bridge_segment.insight: They value convenience in beverages, just not bad coffee

act1.social_agents_insight: Your subscribers will try it out of loyalty, but won't repurchase — it's not their ritual. New audiences will love the product but think 'craft coffee' means pretentious and overpriced.

new_audiences.name: Iced Coffee Addicts
new_audiences.psychographics: Cold coffee is a lifestyle, not seasonal, brand-curious

affinities.name: Coffee Shop Reviews
affinities.reason: Constantly seeking the best cold brew

new_audiences.recommended_angle: Best canned coffee you've ever had

strategies.label: Premium Convenience
strategies.description: Target the gateway segment with a craft + convenience pitch. Position RTD as Ember quality, made convenient.
strategies.target: Gateway segment (8K existing subscribers who value convenience)
strategies.risks: Limited reach, subscribers may still see it as a downgrade

strategies.label: Best Canned Coffee Ever
strategies.description: Target cold audiences who've never heard of Ember. Make them fall in love with the can first, the brand second.
strategies.target: Cold audiences - Commuters, Iced Coffee Addicts, Office Snackers
strategies.pitch: Cold brew so good you'll cancel your coffee shop run
strategies.risks: Brand dilution risk, craft audience may feel alienated

act2.title: Creative Bake-off
act2.question: Our whole-bean aesthetic won't sell a $6 can.
act2.context: 12 creatives submitted. LCBM scores against both audience tracks.

creatives.name: Roaster's Pick
creatives.description: Head roaster taste-testing cans behind the scenes
creatives.why_it_works: Existing subscribers trust the roaster. Authenticity drives repurchase.
creatives.why_it_fails: Cold audiences don't know Ember. Heritage story means nothing yet.

creatives.name: Morning Shortcut
creatives.description: Woman grabs a can, skips the coffee shop line, $4 saved
creatives.hook: Cold brew so good you'll cancel your coffee shop run
creatives.why_it_works: Relatable, saves money, zero pretension. Speaks to commuters perfectly.
creatives.why_it_fails: Subscribers feel their ritual is being reduced to convenience.

creatives.name: Single Origin Story
creatives.description: The Yirgacheffe origin story — farm to can
creatives.hook: From Ethiopian highlands to your morning commute
creatives.why_it_works: Coffee nerds love provenance. Yirgacheffe is a magic word for them.
creatives.why_it_fails: Cold audiences don't know what Yirgacheffe means and don't care.

creatives.name: Gym Grab
creatives.description: Pre-workout grab from the fridge, quick sip, power through the workout
creatives.hook: Your pre-workout just got a serious upgrade
creatives.why_it_works: Fitness crowd loves performance framing. Simple and energetic.
creatives.why_it_fails: Heritage subscribers don't identify with gym culture coffee.

creatives.name: The 3PM Savior
creatives.description: Can on desk, afternoon slump solved, productivity restored
creatives.hook: The 3PM slump just met its match
creatives.why_it_works: Universal workplace moment. Instantly relatable to office workers.
creatives.why_it_fails: Too generic for craft coffee subscribers who have a ritual.

creatives.name: Blind Taste Test
creatives.description: People blind taste-test vs major cold brew brands — Ember wins every time
creatives.hook: We let 100 strangers decide. The results shocked us.
creatives.why_it_works: Social proof + curiosity hook. Works across audiences.
creatives.why_it_fails: Subscribers already know Ember is good. This preaches to the choir.

premium_convenience.landing_page: Craft story page — subscribers see the heritage journey

best_canned_coffee.landing_page: Simple try-it page — no craft jargon, just the product

act3.title: Performance Diagnosis
act3.question: New customers love it. Our own subscribers won't reorder.
act3.context: Five weeks in. The data is in. Something unexpected happened.

tracks.name: Existing Subscribers
tracks.insight: Tried once out of loyalty, won't come back

diagnosis.title: Format Mismatch Detected
diagnosis.not_the_problem: Not creative fatigue — frequency is only 2.1
diagnosis.root_cause: LCBM detects subscribers see canned coffee as a downgrade, not an addition. The ads position it as 'Ember in a can' — which reads as 'lesser version of what I already have'
diagnosis.funnel_insight: Subscribers click, buy once (loyalty), but post-purchase survey shows: 'I prefer my usual pour-over'
diagnosis.social_agents: Stop selling them a replacement. Sell them a COMPANION — 'For when you can't brew.' It's their Ember for the airport, the office, the road trip.

recovery_strategies.label: Companion Not Replacement
recovery_strategies.description: Reframe the can as the travel/office companion to the pour-over ritual, not a replacement
recovery_strategies.transsuasion_copy: Your pour-over ritual. Everywhere else, this.
recovery_strategies.bundle_idea: Subscriber-exclusive bundle: beans + cans at a discount

recovery_strategies.label: Double Down on New Audiences
recovery_strategies.description: Stop subscriber push entirely. Reallocate all budget to new audiences who are performing at 3.1x ROAS
recovery_strategies.transsuasion_copy: The cold brew that made 10,000 commuters cancel their Starbucks order

tracks.name: New Audiences
tracks.insight: Best performing campaign in Ember history

tracks.name: Existing Subscribers
tracks.insight: Product-first messaging doesn't resonate with craft loyalists

diagnosis.title: Audience Identity Conflict
diagnosis.not_the_problem: New audience performance is excellent — don't touch it
diagnosis.root_cause: The product-first messaging that works brilliantly for cold audiences actively alienates existing subscribers who bought into the craft story
diagnosis.funnel_insight: Subscribers open ads, see convenience messaging, feel their identity as 'serious coffee people' is threatened
diagnosis.social_agents: You have two completely different products for two completely different people. Stop trying to serve both with one message. Segment hard.

recovery_strategies.label: Hard Segmentation
recovery_strategies.description: Run completely separate campaigns. Exclude all existing subscribers from new audience campaigns entirely
recovery_strategies.transsuasion_copy: Subscribers: 'Your ritual, now in a can.' New audiences: 'Best cold brew you've ever had.'
recovery_strategies.expected_lift: Subscriber repeat rate 7% → 18%, New audience ROAS 3.1x → 3.8x

recovery_strategies.label: Abandon Subscriber Push
recovery_strategies.description: Subscribers will organically discover the can. Focus 100% budget on new audience acquisition
recovery_strategies.transsuasion_copy: The cold brew that converts Starbucks regulars to craft coffee lovers

act4.title: Regional Expansion
act4.question: We're DTC-only in Portland. Time to go retail in 3 new markets.
act4.context: Ember's cold brew cans are ready for retail distribution. But the RTD competitive landscape is different in each market.

markets.name: Austin
markets.competitive_landscape: Dominated by local brand Chameleon Cold Brew
markets.lcbm_insight: Lead with single-origin positioning — Austin's specialty coffee culture responds to provenance. Unlike DTC cold audiences, Austin responds to WHERE the coffee comes from.

playbook.awareness: Single-origin story ads targeting local Austin coffee culture
playbook.consideration: Comparison content vs Chameleon — quality differentiation
playbook.conversion: Drive to H-E-B and Whole Foods retail locations

companion_positioning.advantage: Subscriber base in Austin area becomes brand advocates in retail
companion_positioning.extra_tactic: Local subscriber meetup + in-store sampling event

new_customer.advantage: Cold audience playbook already proven in DTC transfers to retail
new_customer.extra_tactic: QR codes on cans linking to DTC subscription offer

markets.name: Denver
markets.lcbm_insight: Outdoor and active lifestyle dominates. The Fitness-Before-Work segment is 55% larger in Denver than the national average. Lead with the convenience-for-active-people angle.

playbook.awareness: Active lifestyle content — trails, gyms, outdoor workouts
playbook.consideration: Partner content with Denver hiking and outdoor brands
playbook.conversion: Drive to REI Co-op and local grocery chains

companion_positioning.advantage: Denver subscribers are likely outdoor enthusiasts — perfect companion use case
companion_positioning.extra_tactic: Trail run sponsorship with Ember cans as post-run refreshment

new_customer.advantage: Fitness crowd already primed from DTC campaigns
new_customer.extra_tactic: Partner with Denver fitness influencers for in-store launch

markets.name: Brooklyn
markets.competitive_landscape: Saturated — highest competition but highest willingness-to-pay
markets.specialty_coffee_culture: Very strong — ingredient and origin savvy
markets.lcbm_insight: Brooklyn's audience DOES care about Yirgacheffe, unlike the national DTC audience. The roaster story resonates here. But premium fatigue is real — emphasize value-per-ounce vs competitors.

playbook.awareness: Roaster heritage story — Ember's origin and craft process
playbook.consideration: Value comparison — Ember can vs daily coffee shop spend
playbook.conversion: Drive to local Brooklyn specialty stores and DTC

companion_positioning.advantage: Brooklyn craft coffee lovers are the ideal subscriber → can crossover
companion_positioning.extra_tactic: Pop-up at Brooklyn coffee festivals with subscription bundle offer

new_customer.advantage: Brooklyn's coffee sophistication means product quality sells itself
new_customer.extra_tactic: Blind taste test events vs premium RTD competitors

layers.name: DTC Retargeting
layers.description: People who bought online get targeted with retail availability ads

layers.name: Retail Awareness
layers.description: New local audiences who've never bought Ember online

layers.name: Brand Building
layers.description: City-specific content building local brand love
